message:
fix(openclaw): use multi-agent detection in detect() instead of generic path probe

The detect() method was using franken_detection_for_connector() which only
checks for directory existence at ~/.openclaw and ~/.openclaw/agents. This
could report false even when sessions exist, because it doesn't walk the
agents/<name>/sessions/ layout. Now detect() uses the existing
detect_from_agents_root() method that properly discovers agent session
directories, aligning detect() with scan() behavior.

Also fixes the hardcoded wrong path in default_probe_paths_tilde() which
referenced ~/.openclaw/agents/openclaw/sessions (single-agent assumption)
instead of ~/.openclaw/agents (the multi-agent root).

Fixes Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search#86

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -509,7 +509,7 @@ pub fn default_probe_paths_tilde() -> Vec<(&'static str, Vec<String>)> {
                 ],
                 "goose" => vec![tilde(&[".goose", "sessions"])],
                 "opencode" => vec![tilde(&[".local", "share", "opencode"])],
-                "openclaw" => vec![tilde(&[".openclaw", "agents", "openclaw", "sessions"])],
+                "openclaw" => vec![tilde(&[".openclaw", "agents"])],
                 "pi_agent" => vec![tilde(&[".pi", "agent", "sessions"])],
                 "vibe" => vec![tilde(&[".vibe", "logs", "session"])],
                 "windsurf" => vec![tilde(&[".windsurf"])],
